# UC-18.2.1 · VMware NSX Distributed Firewall Rule-Hit Trending, Microsegmentation Effectiveness, and East-West Allow/Drop Visibility

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Security, Performance, Compliance, Inventory, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch which tiny rules welcome or refuse messages between guest computers on your grounds. Sudden swings in allow counts versus deny counts surface early so nobody blames applications when shifting security posture hides behind silence.*

---

## Description

Tracks VMware NSX **Distributed Firewall** verdict telemetry emitted by **`vsip`** rule evaluation at guest **vNIC** edges — **`INET match ALLOW|DROP|REJECT`** narratives shaped like **`dfwpktlogs`** fingerprints — parses **`RULE_TAG`** tuples (**`sequence|security-policy-name|numeric-rule-id`**) into **`policy_hint`** joins against **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** snapshots, contrasts **`ALLOW`** counters versus **`DROP`/`REJECT`** streaks across **`IPv4`/`IPv6`**, overlays **Identity Firewall** **`USERNAME`** attribution plus **`APP_ID_*`** Layer-7 hits (**`APP_ID_HTTP`**, **`APP_ID_LDAP`**, **`APP_ID_OFFICE365`** when **`Context Profiles`** attach), correlates **`NSGroup`** churn via **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`**, and exposes drift where **`applied_to`** scope no longer matches intent — distinct from overlay dataplane monitors because kernel-mode **`Distributed Firewall`** policy stays authoritative on east-west tuples regardless of upstream routing posture.

## Value

East-west compromises rarely advertise themselves through heroic syslog banners — they whisper inside permissive **`ALLOW`** streaks missing sober **`DROP`** context until auditors knock. Folding **`RULE_TAG`** lineage, **`policy_hint_latest`** labels, **`east_dst_deny_cardinality`**, **`last_user_context`**, **`l7_profile_vals`**, and **`tcp_flag_latest`** into Splunk lets zero-trust squads retire brittle spreadsheet governance, rehearse **`security-policies`** sequencing before **`DEFAULT_DENY`** narratives regress, prove Identity Firewall coverage during VDI churn, validate **`cross-vCenter`** **`Distributed Firewall`** synchronization narratives during stretched **`Domain`** deployments, and catch **`APP_ID`** gaps before Layer-7 posture silently slides toward Layer-4-only allowances.

## Implementation

Deploy **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** syslog ingestion targeting **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** (add optional isolated **`vmware:nsxt:idfw`** when **`USERNAME`** parsing needs bespoke **`props.conf`** overlays); schedule HTTPS collectors emitting **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** plus **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** JSON mirroring **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/security-policies`**, **`.../{policy-id}/rules`**, and **`.../groups`**; join **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** guest inventory for friendly labels; normalize **`RULE_TAG`** extractions and **`FIELDALIAS`** chains inside a governed custom app; size Splunk Connect for Syslog receivers for five-digit EPS bursts tied to **`Connection`/`Drop`/`Reject`/`Information`** logging modes.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) where **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** parsing executes for VMware NSX 4.2, supported VMware NSX 4.1, or NSX-T 3.2.x estates aligned with Broadcom lifecycle guidance.
- **Hypervisor coverage** — Forward `/var/log/dfwpktlogs.log` plus complementary **`vmkernel.log`** excerpts from ESXi hosts running the **`vsip`** dvfilter chain described in VMware **Distributed Firewall** logging references ([Knowledge Article 86365](https://knowledge.broadcom.com/external/article?legacyId=86365)).
- **REST snapshots** — Schedule HTTPS collectors polling **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/security-policies`**, **`GET .../{policy-id}/rules`**, and **`GET .../groups`** into **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** + **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** JSON keyed by **`RULE_TAG`**, **`applied_to`**, **`profiles`**, and **`services`** fields so Splunk lookups survive nightly churn.
- **Identity pairing** — Deploy **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when VM display names must enrich bare IPv4/IPv6 literals tied to **`USERNAME`** Identity Firewall attribution.
- **RBAC personas** — Collector identities still require **`infra`** read scopes plus **`policy`** segments covering **`security-policies`** endpoints—follow VMware RBAC matrices rather than interactive admin credentials.
- **EPS budgeting** — Size syslog receivers (Splunk Connect for Syslog clusters or Heavy Forwarders) because **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** bursts routinely exceed ten thousand EPS during **`microsegmentation`** exercises.

**Step 1 — Wire ingestion**
(1) **Syslog forwarding** — Aim ESXi syslog streams carrying **`INET match ALLOW|DROP|REJECT`** narratives toward Splunk TCP/TLS ingest; preserve **`PRI`** framing consistent with RFC 3164 / RFC 5424 so TA extractions populate **`facility`** / **`severity`** / sourcetype routing.
(2) **Manager relay option** — Optionally aggregate through NSX Manager syslog relays—verify substrings such as **`UNCAST-DOMAIN`** stay intact before **`dfwpktlogs`** parsing.
(3) **Custom policy index** — Emit **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** lines with each rule’s **`tag`**, **`display_name`**, **`action`**, **`logged`**, **`direction`**, **`ip_protocol`**, and service references—this is what makes **`RULE_TAG`** correlation auditable offline.
(4) **Group membership snapshots** — Mirror **`groups`** REST output so **`NSGroup`** churn visible in Splunk precedes unexplained **`applied_to`** explosions.
(5) **Multi-Manager tagging** — When **cross-vCenter** stretched fabrics synchronize **Universal** domains, stamp `site_id`/`manager_cluster` metadata on collectors to disambiguate duplicate policy names across regions.

**Step 2 — Author macros and embed SPL**
Promote the **`spl`** payload into a saved search once indexes and sourcetypes align with your **`props.conf`** overlays. Factor repeated **`eval`** logic into **`macros/nsxt_dfw_hit`** so threshold tuning fans out fleet-wide. Keep the macro under version control beside the saved search so quarterly audits replay the exact **`eval`** semantics.

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:idfw")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action, action, firewall_action, match_action, "")))
| eval dfw_action_norm=case(
    match(verdict,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$"), "ALLOW",
    match(verdict,"^(DROP|DENY)$"), "DROP",
    match(verdict,"^(REJECT|RESET)$"), "REJECT",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bALLOW\b"), "ALLOW",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bDROP\b"), "DROP",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bREJECT\b"), "REJECT",
    1=1, verdict)
| eval rule_tag_raw=trim(coalesce(rule_tag, RULE_TAG, ruleTag, rule_tag_string, ""))
| rex field=rule_tag_raw "^(?<seq_hint>[^|]+)\|(?<policy_hint>[^|]+)\|(?<rule_numeric>[^|]+)$"
| eval src_ip=trim(coalesce(src_ip, src_addr, cip, src, ""))
| eval dst_ip=trim(coalesce(dst_ip, dst_addr, dip, dest_ip, ""))
| eval src_pt=tonumber(coalesce(src_port, sport, sport_number, "0"))
| eval dst_pt=tonumber(coalesce(dst_port, dport, dport_number, "0"))
| eval proto=upper(trim(coalesce(protocol, proto, ip_proto, "TCP")))
| eval vnic_mark=trim(coalesce(vnic_port_id, dvport_id, port_key, lp_port_id, ""))
| eval idfw_user=trim(coalesce(USERNAME, user_name, idfw_principal, sso_username, ""))
| eval app_id_mark=trim(coalesce(app_id, APP_ID, l7_application_id, context_profile_hit, ""))
| eval tcp_sig=trim(coalesce(tcp_flags, flags, tcp_flag, flag_bits, ""))
| eval ip_family=case(match(src_ip,"^[0-9a-f:]+(:[0-9a-f:]+)+$"),"IPV6",match(src_ip,"^[0-9.]+$"),"IPV4",1=1,"MIXED")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)INET\s+match\s+(?<pkt_inet_action>ALLOW|DROP|REJECT)"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as hit_volume,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="ALLOW",1,0))) as allow_hits,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="DROP",1,0))) as drop_hits,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="REJECT",1,0))) as reject_hits,
       dc(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="ALLOW",src_ip,null()))) as east_src_allow_cardinality,
       dc(eval(if(match(dfw_action_norm,"DROP|REJECT"),dst_ip,null()))) as east_dst_deny_cardinality,
       latest(policy_hint) as policy_hint_latest,
       values(app_id_mark) as l7_profile_vals,
       latest(idfw_user) as last_user_context,
       values(vnic_mark) as vnic_samples,
       latest(tcp_sig) as tcp_flag_latest,
       latest(pkt_inet_action) as inet_raw_action
    by _time rule_tag_raw dfw_action_norm ip_family
| eval effectiveness_ratio=round(if((drop_hits+reject_hits)==0,null(),allow_hits/(allow_hits+drop_hits+reject_hits)),4)
| sort -hit_volume
| table _time rule_tag_raw policy_hint_latest dfw_action_norm ip_family allow_hits drop_hits reject_hits hit_volume east_src_allow_cardinality east_dst_deny_cardinality l7_profile_vals last_user_context tcp_flag_latest inet_raw_action effectiveness_ratio
```

**Search logic notes**
- The search normalizes `ALLOW` / `DROP` / `REJECT` verdicts (`dfw_action_norm`) either from extracted fields or from `_raw` **`INET match`** strings.
- **`rule_tag_raw`** carries the **`sequence|policy-name|numeric-id`** tuple that maps to **`security-policies`** / **`rules`** API objects—treat this as your primary join key back to **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** snapshots.
- `east_src_allow_cardinality` and `east_dst_deny_cardinality` approximate east-west chatter breadth—pair with **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** lookups when **`applied_to`** scopes widen because group membership shifted.
- `last_user_context` captures Identity Firewall results; NULL does not mean benign—see false-positive notes for directory failovers.
- `app_id_mark` retains NSX **4.x** Layer-7 markers such as `APP_ID_HTTP`, `APP_ID_LDAP`, `APP_ID_OFFICE365` when **`Context Profiles`** attach to **`services`**.
- **`inet_raw_action`** rolls up the **`INET match`** verb beside **`tcp_flag_latest`** so parser drift surfaces before blaming workload owners.

**Step 3 — Validate in VMware consoles**
**Verify against NSX Manager Policy UI** whenever Splunk aggregates disagree with VMware screenshots captured during incidents.

(1) **`RULE_TAG` parity** — Compare Splunk rule_numeric and policy_hint extractions against NSX Manager Policy UI **Distributed Firewall** tabs for the same domain so field names stay aligned.
(2) **Inventory alignment** — Cross-check east-west IPs with vCenter inventories; mismatches usually trace to stale lookups rather than VMware itself.
(3) **Identity drilldown** — When **`USERNAME`** blanks appear, inspect **NSX-Identity-Service** integration health and Active Directory replication rather than Splunk parsers first.
(4) **`APP_ID` truth** — Validate Layer-7 counts using **Monitor → Traceflow / Live Traffic** captures that prove **`context-profiles`** bindings.
(5) **Group deltas** — Align deny spikes with **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** nightlies that show **`NSGroup`** membership transitions.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) **Alert routing** — Send sustained **`DROP` / `reject_hits`** bursts to **`security-policies`** owners; send unusual **`ALLOW`** jumps to application teams.
(2) **Suppressions** — Tie maintenance windows from **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** evacuations to Splunk suppression lists so host-draining storms do not page unnecessarily.
(3) **Runbooks** — Store NSX **`security-policies`** export hashes next to Splunk screenshots for audit traceability.
(4) **Dashboards** — Keep per-tenant tiles for **`policy_hint_latest`** versus global rollups to avoid masking a single noisy section.
(5) **SC4S tuning** — Balance batching/ack settings on high-volume syslog routes so ingestion delay is not mistaken for missing firewall hits.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

- **Quiet firewall sourcetype windows** — Validate Universal Forwarders still monitor active dfwpktlogs paths; rotated gzip bundles skipped by stale monitor configuration silently zero-out vmware:nsxt:firewall rows until someone notices dashboards fade—when rows vanish entirely, missing fields reported in fieldsummary usually implicate stale monitor paths before anything else.

- **Odd RULE_TAG tokenization** — Real `_raw` samples occasionally diverge from sequence|policy|id templates; extend rex carefully after capturing operator conventions rather than forcing brittle splits.

- **NULL effectiveness_ratio math** — The ratio intentionally drops NULL when deny counters sum to zero—keep informational tiles separate from paging triggers.

- **Blank USERNAME columns** — Identity Firewall connectors pause during LDAP saturation or NSX-Identity-Service maintenance—correlate with directory alarms before rewriting Splunk FIELDALIAS stanzas.

- **Missing APP_ID strings** — Pure Layer-4 rules lack Layer-7 profiles; Splunk faithfully mirrors absent APP_ID markers—confirm Policy UI attachments before accusing parsers.

- **Stale policy_hint snapshots** — Hourly HTTPS collectors lag rapid edits—tighten poll cadence during migration weekends.

- **Duplicate cross-vCenter flows** — Universal synchronization may double counts briefly—dedupe using locale identifiers captured inside REST payloads.

- **IPv6 categorization quirks** — Compressed literals confuse naive IPv4 detectors—normalize addresses upstream when audits demand strict ip_family tagging.

- **vsip backlog chatter** — Kernel dvfilter stalls belong in VMware support tickets—do not mask them by muting Splunk alerts indefinitely.

- **TA upgrade fallout** — Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt upgrades occasionally rename extraction keys—diff props overlays immediately after maintenance windows.

- **PRI stripping relays** — Syslog proxies that strip bracketed priorities break TA routing—enforce RFC-preserving relays end-to-end.

- **RST noise versus reject_hits** — REJECT verdicts emit ICMP or TCP resets—confirm via PCAP whether resets originate inside guests versus firewall enforcement.

- **ANY-group cardinality explosions** — Policies referencing overly broad NSGroup scopes inflate uniqueness metrics—audit nightly vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups extracts before blaming cardinality limits.

- **Logged-mode EPS spikes** — Operators toggling Connection versus Information versus Drop logging multiply syslog volume—coordinate observability modes before paging denial narratives.



## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:idfw")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action, action, firewall_action, match_action, "")))
| eval dfw_action_norm=case(
    match(verdict,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$"), "ALLOW",
    match(verdict,"^(DROP|DENY)$"), "DROP",
    match(verdict,"^(REJECT|RESET)$"), "REJECT",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bALLOW\b"), "ALLOW",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bDROP\b"), "DROP",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\bREJECT\b"), "REJECT",
    1=1, verdict)
| eval rule_tag_raw=trim(coalesce(rule_tag, RULE_TAG, ruleTag, rule_tag_string, ""))
| rex field=rule_tag_raw "^(?<seq_hint>[^|]+)\|(?<policy_hint>[^|]+)\|(?<rule_numeric>[^|]+)$"
| eval src_ip=trim(coalesce(src_ip, src_addr, cip, src, ""))
| eval dst_ip=trim(coalesce(dst_ip, dst_addr, dip, dest_ip, ""))
| eval src_pt=tonumber(coalesce(src_port, sport, sport_number, "0"))
| eval dst_pt=tonumber(coalesce(dst_port, dport, dport_number, "0"))
| eval proto=upper(trim(coalesce(protocol, proto, ip_proto, "TCP")))
| eval vnic_mark=trim(coalesce(vnic_port_id, dvport_id, port_key, lp_port_id, ""))
| eval idfw_user=trim(coalesce(USERNAME, user_name, idfw_principal, sso_username, ""))
| eval app_id_mark=trim(coalesce(app_id, APP_ID, l7_application_id, context_profile_hit, ""))
| eval tcp_sig=trim(coalesce(tcp_flags, flags, tcp_flag, flag_bits, ""))
| eval ip_family=case(match(src_ip,"^[0-9a-f:]+(:[0-9a-f:]+)+$"),"IPV6",match(src_ip,"^[0-9.]+$"),"IPV4",1=1,"MIXED")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)INET\s+match\s+(?<pkt_inet_action>ALLOW|DROP|REJECT)"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as hit_volume,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="ALLOW",1,0))) as allow_hits,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="DROP",1,0))) as drop_hits,
       sum(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="REJECT",1,0))) as reject_hits,
       dc(eval(if(dfw_action_norm="ALLOW",src_ip,null()))) as east_src_allow_cardinality,
       dc(eval(if(match(dfw_action_norm,"DROP|REJECT"),dst_ip,null()))) as east_dst_deny_cardinality,
       latest(policy_hint) as policy_hint_latest,
       values(app_id_mark) as l7_profile_vals,
       latest(idfw_user) as last_user_context,
       values(vnic_mark) as vnic_samples,
       latest(tcp_sig) as tcp_flag_latest,
       latest(pkt_inet_action) as inet_raw_action
    by _time rule_tag_raw dfw_action_norm ip_family
| eval effectiveness_ratio=round(if((drop_hits+reject_hits)==0,null(),allow_hits/(allow_hits+drop_hits+reject_hits)),4)
| sort -hit_volume
| table _time rule_tag_raw policy_hint_latest dfw_action_norm ip_family allow_hits drop_hits reject_hits hit_volume east_src_allow_cardinality east_dst_deny_cardinality l7_profile_vals last_user_context tcp_flag_latest inet_raw_action effectiveness_ratio
```

## Visualization

(1) Stacked timechart **`allow_hits`** beside **`drop_hits`** and **`reject_hits`** faceted by **`policy_hint_latest`**. (2) Heatmap **`east_dst_deny_cardinality`** × **`rule_tag_raw`** split by **`ip_family`**. (3) Table pairing **`east_src_allow_cardinality`** with **`east_dst_deny_cardinality`** beside **`last_user_context`** rows for VM-to-VM posture review. (4) Single-value **`effectiveness_ratio`** when deny plus reject dominates steady workloads. (5) Bubble density for **`l7_profile_vals`** contrasting **`APP_ID_*`** adoption versus Layer-4-only flows.

## Known False Positives

**Workload shuffle bursts** — Planned guest migrations briefly rebuild distributed-firewall flow journals while **vsip** state tables refill on the landing ESXi host; **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** events-per-second climbs although **`ALLOW`** verdict continuity resumes moments later—suppress brief spikes anchored to **`CMDB`** relocation stamps rather than lateral-move hunts. **Identity-mapping blanks** — Active Directory rollover intervals yield **`USERNAME`** gaps while **`NSX-Identity-Service`** caches heal; **`DROP`** counters remain truthful yet human-readable attribution dips—coordinate paging windows with **`domain-controller`** bridge calendars before accusing spoofed lateral phishing. **Legacy-to-current uplift duplication** — In-place uplift weekends replay **`Distributed Firewall`** sections while VMware reformats Policy domains toward four-dot-zero planes; syslog volume doubles briefly until **`Broadcom`** reconciliation completes—extend SPL joins across stabilization intervals only. **Operator tagging churn** — Teams renaming **`RULE_TAG`** strings mid-migration (`policy-name` edits without mirrored Splunk lookups) skew dashboards referencing stale **`rule_tag_raw`** aliases until nightly **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** snapshots refresh lookup tables—expect benign spikes whenever admins refactor tagging conventions alone. **Emergency deny rehearsals** — Temporary **`DROP`** surges surface while inserting interim **`JUMP_TO_APPLICATION`** placeholders during modernization waves—confirm **`rule_numeric`** increments align with **`CRQ`** evidence before paging application owners. **Maintenance envelopes** — Entering **`host`** **`maintenance`** stalls fresh **`dfwpktlogs`** journal lines from that hypervisor tail, mimicking faux silence until **`DRS`** completes relocation—cross-check **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** relocation narratives.
